criteria.pass: Returns exactly one fast, standard, full, or blocked route with evidence-based reasons and one next capability or blocking action; it does not mix routes or start downstream work.
criteria.fail: Returns multiple competing routes, omits the route, chooses a route without reasons, or starts writing documents or production files while classifying.

criteria.pass: Chooses fast only when implementation is explicitly approved, the goal and observable result are stable, the change is local and ordinarily reversible, no listed high-risk boundary is present, focused validation is sufficient, and repository rules do not require a stronger path; it selects implementing-bounded-changes without implementing the change itself.
criteria.fail: Chooses fast while any condition is missing, invents approval, or performs the requested edit instead of handing off.

criteria.pass: Chooses standard for an ordinary feature that needs product and technical clarification but has no confirmed full-route risk, and also fails closed to standard rather than fast when a material routing fact is unknown; it asks only the decisive missing question when clarification is required.
criteria.fail: Routes an ordinary or materially uncertain feature to fast, escalates to full without a high-risk fact, or asks an unrelated batch of questions.

criteria.pass: Chooses full when the request changes a data model and permission boundary, names the observed risk facts, preserves the complete PRD-spec-plan gates, and does not implement or design the migration while routing.
criteria.fail: Chooses fast or standard for the confirmed high-risk change, weakens the existing gates, or begins migration design or implementation.

criteria.pass: Reports that the explicit creating-product-requirements entry is outside this router's applicability, preserves that requested capability, and emits no workflow_route or canonical router handoff while creating no PRD itself.
criteria.fail: Invokes the router for the explicit entry, emits workflow_route including a bypass value or competing route, overrides the requested capability, creates the PRD, or searches fixed installation paths.

criteria.pass: Emits one compact canonical handoff containing one fast, standard, full, or blocked route, scope summary, observed risk facts including unknowns, approval state, destination capability, and next action; downstream capability selection uses runtime exposure rather than sibling source or installation paths.
criteria.fail: Leaves the handoff implicit, drops unknown or approval state, fabricates a capability, or reads/calls a sibling through a repository, installation, or plugin-cache path.
